report_name: Microsoft's Record-Breaking June Patch Tuesday Fixes 206 Flaws, Including 3 Zero-Days

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-50507
vulnerabilities.description: A security feature bypass vulnerability in Windows BitLocker that allows an attacker with physical access to a device to bypass BitLocker Device Encryption and gain access to encrypted data.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-49160
vulnerabilities.description: A denial-of-service vulnerability in HTTP.sys that allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to cause a denial-of-service by sending specially crafted HTTP/2 packets in a 'HTTP/2 Bomb' attack.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-45586
vulnerabilities.description: A local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability in the Windows Collaborative Translation Framework that allows a low-privileged attacker who has already gained access to a system to gain SYSTEM-level privileges.

indicators.name: Suspicious Child Process of ctfmon.exe
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Child Process of ctfmon.exe
id: 7b8a1c2d-3e4f-4a5b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: 'Detects command shells (cmd.exe, powershell.exe) being spawned as a child process of ctfmon.exe. This activity is anomalous and could indicate exploitation of CVE-2026-45586, a privilege escalation vulnerability in the Windows Collaborative Translation Framework.'
references:
  - https://www.example.com/microsoft-june-2026-patch-tuesday-fixes-206-vulnerabilities-three-zero-days
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-11
tags:
  - attack.t1068
  - attack.privilege_escalation
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\ctfmon.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects command shells (cmd.exe, powershell.exe) spawning from ctfmon.exe, a potential indicator of exploitation of the privilege escalation vulnerability CVE-2026-45586.
